observations: The main.py content reveals the primary orchestration of fetching news items, filtering unseen URLs, scoring, generating drafts, and sending emails or printing reports.
The pyproject.toml confirms dependencies and reveals some optional dev dependencies and pytest configuration.
The src/config.py provides a detailed Settings dataclass with environment-based loading and properties indicating generation and email sending readiness.
No README.md exists, so significant foundational documentation must be created from code and config information.
Docstrings exist minimally in src/__init__.py and src/publisher.py, and there are many relevant modules under src/fetchers, src/generator.py, src/models.py, src/notifier.py, and src/db.py.

Creating composite tools to extract docstrings and summarize key source modules in src/fetchers/, src/generator.py, src/db.py, src/models.py, and src/notifier.py will enable forming component summaries for documentation.

Plan to extract key functional docstrings for components, summarize setup and usage from pyproject.toml and main.py, and then draft a documentation plan covering setup, usage, component overviews, and API references.
A documentation evaluation rubric will be useful to check coverage of entrypoints, setup instructions, component explanations, and hallucination avoidance in generated docs.

extract_docstrings_and_comments.description: Extracts the top-level docstrings and comments from Python files to summarize the file purpose.
extract_docstrings_and_comments.synthesis: Parse the file content to extract the module-level docstring or initial comment block at the top of the file to produce a summary snippet indicating the purpose and main features of the file.

skills.analyze_module_summaries: Use extract_docstrings_and_comments on the main source modules and fetchers to gather summaries; review their outputs to understand component roles and their responsibilities in the system.
skills.develop_doc_plan: Combine information from pyproject.toml (for setup and dependencies), main.py (for entrypoint usage), config.py (for configuration overview), and extracted module summaries to draft a documentation plan that covers setup instructions, usage, component architecture overview, and API references.

workflow: Use extract_docstrings_and_comments on main functional source files to gather source-level summaries.
Review relevant configuration and setup files for setup instructions and environment variable configurations.
Analyze results to draft a comprehensive documentation plan.
Refine skills to automate extraction and generation of documentation content based on source summaries.

documents.purpose: Document setup commands, dependencies, and configuration environment variables to guide installing and running the project.
Detail the main usage flow and entrypoint behavior from main.py including how data fetching, scoring, draft generation, and notification are orchestrated.
Provide summaries and explanations of major components like fetchers, database, generator, notifier, and models based on extracted module docstrings.
Catalog public interfaces and classes with signatures and descriptions for developers to reference.
